meta.source: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (BNS) replaced IPC 1860 from 1 July 2024. CrPC 1973 → BNSS 2023. IEA 1872 → BSA 2023.

sections.title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
sections.explanation: When 2+ people share criminal intent and act on it, each is liable as if they did the whole crime alone.
sections.example: Two friends plan a robbery; one drives, one robs — both equally guilty.

sections.title: Voluntarily
sections.explanation: Defines 'voluntarily' — knowing or having reason to believe likely consequences.

sections.title: Injury
sections.explanation: Any harm to body, mind, reputation, or property is injury under IPC/BNS.

sections.title: Good faith
sections.explanation: An act done with 'due care and attention' qualifies as good faith.

sections.title: Act done by person bound by law
sections.explanation: If law requires you to do something, that act is not a crime even if it would otherwise be.
sections.example: Police arresting a wanted criminal — not assault.

sections.title: Act done by person justified by law
sections.explanation: Acts done in good faith believing them to be legally justified are exempt.

sections.title: Accident in doing a lawful act
sections.explanation: Pure accident with no negligence during a lawful act is not a crime.
sections.example: Bullet ricochets at a gun range; no criminal liability.

sections.title: Child under 7 years
sections.explanation: Children below 7 are doli incapax — incapable of crime.

sections.title: Child 7-12 of immature understanding
sections.explanation: Children 7-12 are exempt if their understanding wasn't mature enough to know consequences.

sections.title: Act of person of unsound mind
sections.explanation: If the accused was incapable of knowing nature/wrongness of the act due to mental illness — not guilty.

sections.title: Act of person incapable of judgment by intoxication
sections.explanation: Involuntary intoxication causing inability to know wrongness — exempt. Voluntary drunkenness is NOT a defence.

sections.title: Things done in private defence
sections.explanation: You may use reasonable force to defend your body or property against imminent harm.
sections.example: Pushing back an attacker is not assault.

sections.title: When right of private defence extends to causing death
sections.explanation: You may kill in self-defence only against grievous attacks: rape, murder threat, kidnapping, acid attack, etc.

sections.title: Criminal conspiracy — definition
sections.explanation: Two or more people agreeing to commit a crime = conspiracy, even if not yet executed.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal conspiracy
sections.punishment: Same as the offence conspired; if no specific punishment — up to 6 months / fine
sections.explanation: Penalty for being part of a criminal conspiracy.

sections.title: Waging or attempting to wage war against Government of India
sections.explanation: Armed rebellion or insurrection against the Indian state.

sections.title: Sedition / Acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity
sections.punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 7 years + fine
sections.explanation: Speech/writing inciting hatred against government. Currently under SC review.

sections.title: Unlawful assembly
sections.explanation: 5+ people gathered with common object of crime/intimidation.

sections.title: Punishment for unlawful assembly
sections.explanation: Just being in an unlawful assembly is punishable.

sections.title: Rioting
sections.explanation: Unlawful assembly that uses violence.

sections.title: Punishment for rioting
sections.explanation: Anyone in a rioting mob is liable.

sections.title: Rioting armed with deadly weapon
sections.explanation: Aggravated form of rioting.

sections.title: Every member of unlawful assembly liable for common object
sections.explanation: If 5 people gather and one commits crime — all 5 liable.

sections.title: Promoting enmity between groups on grounds of religion, race etc.
sections.explanation: Hate speech that incites communal hatred.

sections.title: Imputations prejudicial to national integration
sections.explanation: False claims about communities harming national unity.

sections.title: Public servant disobeying law with intent to cause injury
sections.explanation: Government employee intentionally breaking rules to harm someone.

sections.title: Public servant framing incorrect document
sections.explanation: Falsifying government records.

sections.title: Punishment for bribery (election)
sections.explanation: Offering/accepting money or gifts for votes.

sections.title: False information to public servant
sections.explanation: Lying to police/officials to cause harm to others — e.g., false FIR.

sections.title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
sections.explanation: Stopping police or officials from doing their job.

sections.title: Disobedience of order by public servant
sections.punishment: Up to 1 month + ₹200 (simple); up to 6 months + ₹1,000 (if causes harm)
sections.explanation: Violating prohibitory orders (Section 144 CrPC/163 BNSS).

sections.title: Giving false evidence
sections.explanation: Knowingly lying under oath.

sections.title: Punishment for false evidence in court
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years + fine (judicial); up to 3 years (other)
sections.explanation: Perjury in legal proceedings.

sections.title: Causing disappearance of evidence / giving false information
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years (if capital offence) + fine
sections.explanation: Destroying evidence to shield offender.

sections.title: False charge with intent to injure
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years + fine; 7 years if capital
sections.explanation: Filing false case to harass — punishable.

sections.title: Harbouring offender
sections.explanation: Hiding wanted criminals from law.

sections.title: Disclosing identity of rape victim
sections.explanation: Publishing name/photo of rape survivors banned to protect dignity.

sections.title: Public nuisance
sections.explanation: Any act causing common injury, danger, or annoyance.

sections.title: Negligent act likely to spread infection of dangerous disease
sections.explanation: Used during COVID for negligence cases.

sections.title: Malignant act likely to spread infection of dangerous disease
sections.explanation: Intentional spread of infectious disease.

sections.title: Rash driving on public way endangering human life
sections.punishment: Up to 6 months + fine (BNS: up to ₹5,000)
sections.explanation: Reckless driving putting others at risk.

sections.title: Sale of obscene books, etc.
sections.explanation: Distribution of obscene material.

sections.title: Obscene acts and songs in public
sections.explanation: Eve-teasing, lewd gestures, profanity in public.

sections.title: Deliberate acts insulting religion
sections.explanation: Outraging religious feelings of any class.

sections.title: Uttering words to wound religious feelings
sections.explanation: Verbal insult to religious sentiment.

sections.title: Culpable homicide — definition
sections.explanation: Causing death with intent OR knowledge of likelihood.

sections.title: Murder — definition
sections.explanation: Culpable homicide is murder if done with specific intent or extreme recklessness.

sections.title: Punishment for murder
sections.explanation: Most serious offence — capital punishment possible in rarest of rare cases (Bachan Singh).

sections.title: Punishment for murder by life convict
sections.punishment: Death (per BNS); IPC version struck down in Mithu
sections.explanation: BNS 104 reintroduces capital punishment for murder by life convicts.

sections.title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine (Part I); up to 10 years + fine (Part II)
sections.explanation: Killing without 'murder' intent — e.g., sudden quarrel, grave provocation.

sections.title: Causing death by negligence
sections.punishment: Up to 5 years + fine. BNS: 7 years if hit-and-run + failure to report.
sections.explanation: Most road accidents are charged here. BNS adds harsher hit-and-run punishment.

sections.title: Dowry death
sections.explanation: Unnatural death of woman within 7 years of marriage with prior cruelty for dowry.

sections.title: Abetment of suicide of child or insane person
sections.punishment: Death / life / up to 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Encouraging suicide of minors or mentally ill.

sections.title: Abetment of suicide
sections.explanation: Instigation or aid that drives a person to suicide.

sections.title: Attempt to murder
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; life if hurt caused; life convict — death or life
sections.explanation: Acting with murder intent — even if victim survives.

sections.title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years + fine; 7 years if hurt
sections.explanation: Less serious than 307.

sections.title: Attempt to commit suicide (decriminalized for mental illness)
sections.punishment: Up to 1 year + fine (excludes those under mental illness per Mental Healthcare Act 2017)
sections.explanation: Mental Healthcare Act 2017 effectively decriminalized attempted suicide for mental illness victims.

sections.title: Hurt — definition
sections.explanation: Any bodily pain, disease, or infirmity.

sections.title: Grievous hurt — definition
sections.explanation: Includes emasculation, permanent privation of sight/hearing, fracture, disfiguration of face, etc.

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
sections.explanation: Simple hurt — slap, push, minor assault.

sections.title: Hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.explanation: Hurt with knife, stick, fire, acid, gun.

sections.title: Punishment for grievous hurt
sections.explanation: Serious injury causing fracture, permanent disability.

sections.title: Grievous hurt by dangerous weapons
sections.explanation: Serious injury with deadly weapon.

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by acid attack
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years up to life + fine (paid to victim)
sections.explanation: Acid attacks specifically criminalized after Laxmi v UoI guidelines.

sections.title: Attempt to acid attack
sections.explanation: Even attempted acid attack is punishable.

sections.title: Hurt to deter public servant from his duty
sections.explanation: Attacking police, doctor, or government worker on duty.

sections.title: Act endangering life or personal safety
sections.explanation: Reckless conduct putting others at risk.

sections.title: Causing hurt by act endangering life
sections.explanation: Rash act causing injury.

sections.title: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life
sections.explanation: Rash act causing serious injury.

sections.title: Wrongful restraint
sections.explanation: Blocking someone's free movement temporarily.

sections.title: Wrongful confinement
sections.explanation: Confining a person to a limited area against their will.

sections.title: Assault — definition
sections.explanation: Gesture/preparation causing reasonable apprehension of force.

sections.title: Punishment for assault or criminal force
sections.explanation: Threatening physical contact.

sections.title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
sections.explanation: Molestation, inappropriate touch, harassment.

sections.title: Sexual harassment
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years + fine (1-3); up to 1 year + fine (4)
sections.explanation: Unwelcome sexual advances, demand for sexual favours, showing pornography, sexually colored remarks.

sections.title: Assault with intent to disrobe a woman
sections.explanation: Stripping a woman, attempting to.

sections.title: Voyeurism
sections.explanation: Watching/photographing woman in private act without consent.

sections.title: Stalking
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years + fine (1st); up to 5 years (2nd)
sections.explanation: Repeatedly following, contacting, or monitoring a woman against her wishes (incl. online).

sections.title: Kidnapping — definition
sections.explanation: From India or from lawful guardianship.

sections.title: Punishment for kidnapping
sections.explanation: Taking away a minor (<18 girl / <16 boy) or lunatic without consent of guardian.

sections.title: Kidnapping for begging
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; life if maimed
sections.explanation: Trafficking children for begging rings.

sections.title: Kidnapping to murder
sections.explanation: Kidnapping with intent to murder or risk of murder.

sections.title: Kidnapping with intent to confine secretly
sections.explanation: Holding kidnapped person secretly.

sections.title: Kidnapping or abducting woman to compel marriage
sections.explanation: Forced marriage cases.

sections.title: Procuration of minor girl
sections.explanation: Procuring minor girl (<18) for forced sex/marriage.

sections.title: Importation of girl from foreign country
sections.explanation: International trafficking of girls.

sections.title: Trafficking of person
sections.punishment: 7 to 10 years + fine (basic); higher for aggravated
sections.explanation: Recruiting, transporting, buying or selling persons for exploitation.

sections.title: Exploitation of trafficked person
sections.explanation: Engaging trafficked person for sexual or other exploitation.

sections.title: Rape — definition
sections.explanation: Sexual intercourse without consent or with consent obtained by force, fear, fraud, intoxication. Marital rape currently exempt — challenged in court.

sections.title: Punishment for rape
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years up to life + fine
sections.explanation: After 2013 Criminal Law Amendment (post-Nirbhaya), minimum jumped to 10 years.

sections.title: Rape causing death or persistent vegetative state
sections.punishment: Minimum 20 years up to life or death
sections.explanation: Aggravated rape resulting in death.

sections.title: Rape of woman under 12 years
sections.punishment: Minimum 20 years up to life or death
sections.explanation: Death penalty possible — POCSO Act overlap.

sections.title: Gang rape
sections.punishment: Minimum 20 years up to life + fine
sections.explanation: Two or more persons raping.

sections.title: Gang rape on woman under 16
sections.explanation: Minimum life sentence.

sections.title: Gang rape on woman under 12
sections.explanation: Death penalty mandatory option.

sections.title: Unnatural offences (read down for consenting adults — Navtej Singh Johar 2018)
sections.punishment: Up to life or up to 10 years + fine (only non-consensual / minors / animals)
sections.explanation: Decriminalized for consensual same-sex adult relations.

sections.title: Theft — definition
sections.explanation: Taking movable property without consent, with intent to deprive permanently.

sections.title: Punishment for theft
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years + fine (BNS: up to community service for petty theft <₹5,000)
sections.explanation: Standard theft punishment. BNS introduces community service for petty theft.

sections.title: Theft in dwelling house
sections.explanation: Theft from home gets higher punishment.

sections.title: Theft by clerk or servant of property in employer's possession
sections.explanation: Employee stealing from employer.

sections.title: Extortion
sections.explanation: Forcing someone to give property by threat.

sections.title: Putting person in fear of injury for extortion
sections.explanation: Threatening for extortion even if no money received.

sections.title: Robbery — definition
sections.explanation: Theft or extortion with use or threat of force.

sections.title: Punishment for robbery
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years + fine; 14 years if on highway between sunset–sunrise
sections.explanation: Highway robbery has aggravated punishment.

sections.title: Dacoity (5+ persons committing robbery)
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Robbery by gang of 5 or more.

sections.title: Dacoity with murder
sections.punishment: Death or life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Murder during dacoity — death penalty possible.

sections.title: Dishonest misappropriation of property
sections.explanation: Keeping property you found, knowing the owner.

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust — definition
sections.explanation: Person entrusted with property dishonestly misuses it.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
sections.explanation: Employees, partners, agents breaching trust.

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust by clerk/servant
sections.explanation: Aggravated form for employees.

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust by public servant
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Corruption — government officials misusing public funds.

sections.title: Dishonestly receiving stolen property
sections.explanation: Knowingly buying or keeping stolen goods.

sections.title: Cheating — definition
sections.explanation: Deceiving someone to make them deliver property or do/omit something.

sections.title: Punishment for cheating
sections.explanation: Simple cheating without delivery of property.

sections.title: Cheating by person bound to protect interest
sections.explanation: Trustees, attorneys cheating beneficiaries.

sections.title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
sections.explanation: Common fraud cases — online fraud, fake schemes. Most commonly invoked.
sections.example: OLX fraud, fake job offers, Ponzi schemes.

sections.title: Mischief — definition
sections.explanation: Causing wrongful loss/damage to property.

sections.title: Mischief causing damage to amount of ₹50 or more
sections.explanation: Damaging property worth >₹50.

sections.title: Mischief by fire or explosive substance
sections.explanation: Arson causing >₹100 damage.

sections.title: Mischief by fire of house, etc.
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Setting fire to a house.

sections.title: Criminal trespass — definition
sections.explanation: Entering property to commit offence, intimidate, insult, or annoy.

sections.title: Criminal trespass
sections.explanation: Simple trespass.

sections.title: House-trespass
sections.explanation: Trespass into dwelling, place of worship, or place of property custody.

sections.title: House-trespass after preparation for hurt
sections.explanation: Coming into house with weapon/intent to hurt.

sections.title: Punishment for house-breaking
sections.explanation: Breaking into a building.

sections.title: House-breaking to commit offence
sections.explanation: Breaking in with criminal intent.

sections.title: House-breaking by night
sections.explanation: Aggravated for night-time burglary.

sections.title: Forgery — definition
sections.explanation: Making false document with intent to cause damage or fraud.

sections.title: Punishment for forgery
sections.explanation: Simple forgery.

sections.title: Forgery of court record or public register
sections.explanation: Forgery of official records.

sections.title: Forgery of valuable security or will
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Forging wills, bonds, share certificates.

sections.title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
sections.explanation: Forgery to cheat.

sections.title: Using as genuine a forged document
sections.explanation: Even using forged doc is offence.

sections.title: Counterfeiting currency notes
sections.punishment: Up to life or up to 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Making fake currency.

sections.title: Using forged currency notes as genuine
sections.punishment: Up to life or 10 years + fine
sections.explanation: Circulating counterfeit notes.

sections.title: Cohabitation by deceiving belief of lawful marriage
sections.explanation: Living as wife/husband by deception.

sections.title: Bigamy
sections.explanation: Second marriage while first is subsisting (subject to personal laws).

sections.title: Cruelty by husband or relatives of husband
sections.explanation: Physical/mental cruelty to wife or dowry-related harassment. Arrest only after Section 41A notice per Arnesh Kumar.

sections.title: Defamation — definition
sections.explanation: Imputation harming someone's reputation. 10 exceptions including truth + public interest.

sections.title: Punishment for defamation
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years + fine; community service added in BNS
sections.explanation: Criminal defamation. SC upheld constitutionality in Subramanian Swamy.

sections.title: Criminal intimidation
sections.explanation: Threatening with injury to person, reputation, or property.

sections.title: Intentional insult provoking breach of peace
sections.explanation: Insults that could lead to violence.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years (simple); up to 7 years (death/grievous hurt threat) + fine
sections.explanation: Threatening someone with consequences.

sections.title: Word, gesture, act intended to insult modesty of woman
sections.explanation: Catcalling, lewd gestures, vulgar songs targeted at women.

sections.title: Attempt to commit offences
sections.punishment: Up to half of full punishment, or fine, or both
sections.explanation: Catch-all for attempts at offences not specifically covered.

special_acts.name: Information Technology Act 2000, Section 66
special_acts.title: Computer-related offences (hacking, identity theft)
special_acts.explanation: Covers hacking, data theft, computer fraud.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 66A
special_acts.title: (STRUCK DOWN by Shreya Singhal 2015)
special_acts.explanation: SC struck down for vagueness; sending offensive messages was over-criminalized.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 66C
special_acts.title: Identity theft / fraudulent use of password/digital signature
special_acts.explanation: Using someone's password, OTP, or digital signature fraudulently.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 66D
special_acts.title: Cheating by personation using computer resource
special_acts.explanation: Online impersonation to cheat — fake profiles, scam calls.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 66E
special_acts.title: Violation of privacy (capturing/transmitting private images)
special_acts.explanation: MMS leak cases, hidden camera shots.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 66F
special_acts.title: Cyber terrorism
special_acts.explanation: Attacking critical infrastructure or threatening national security via computer.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 67
special_acts.title: Publishing obscene material in electronic form
special_acts.punishment: Up to 3 years + ₹5 lakh (1st); 5 years + ₹10 lakh (2nd)
special_acts.explanation: Pornography distribution online.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 67A
special_acts.title: Sexually explicit material in electronic form
special_acts.explanation: Explicit sexual content online.

special_acts.name: IT Act, Section 67B
special_acts.title: Child pornography
special_acts.explanation: CSAM offences. Even possession is offence per Just Rights for Children Alliance 2024.

special_acts.name: Negotiable Instruments Act 1881, Section 138
special_acts.title: Dishonour of cheque for insufficient funds
special_acts.punishment: Up to 2 years OR double the cheque amount + fine
special_acts.explanation: Cheque bouncing is criminal. Procedure: return memo → notice within 30 days → wait 15 days → file complaint within 30 days.

special_acts.name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005, Section 12
special_acts.title: Application for protection / residence / monetary relief
special_acts.punishment: Civil reliefs (protection order, monetary, custody, residence)
special_acts.explanation: DV Act provides civil remedies — protection order, residence in shared household, monetary relief, custody.

special_acts.name: DV Act 2005, Section 18
special_acts.title: Protection Orders
special_acts.punishment: Civil order; breach = up to 1 year + ₹20,000
special_acts.explanation: Court order preventing the respondent from contact, threats, alienation of assets.

special_acts.name: POCSO Act 2012, Section 7
special_acts.title: Sexual assault on child (touch with sexual intent)
special_acts.explanation: Any sexual touching of minor.

special_acts.name: POCSO Act 2012, Section 9
special_acts.title: Aggravated sexual assault on child
special_acts.explanation: By police, public servant, family member, etc.

special_acts.name: POCSO Act 2012, Section 11
special_acts.title: Sexual harassment of child
special_acts.explanation: Showing pornography, repeatedly following, words/gestures.

special_acts.name: POCSO Act 2012, Section 14
special_acts.title: Use of child for pornographic purposes
special_acts.explanation: Child involved in any pornographic creation.

special_acts.name: POCSO Act 2012, Section 21
special_acts.title: Punishment for failure to report POCSO offence
special_acts.explanation: Mandatory reporting — failing is offence.

special_acts.name: Motor Vehicles Act 1988, Section 184
special_acts.title: Driving dangerously
special_acts.punishment: Up to 6 months + ₹1,000-5,000 (1st); up to 2 years + ₹10,000 (2nd)
special_acts.explanation: Rash/dangerous driving including using mobile.

special_acts.name: Motor Vehicles Act 1988, Section 185
special_acts.title: Drunken driving (>30mg/100ml blood)
special_acts.explanation: Blood alcohol limit and consequences.

special_acts.name: Motor Vehicles Act 1988, Section 194D
special_acts.title: Riding without helmet / driving without seatbelt
special_acts.explanation: Helmet/seatbelt mandatory.

special_acts.name: Motor Vehicles Act 1988, Section 196
special_acts.title: Driving uninsured vehicle
special_acts.explanation: Third-party insurance mandatory.

special_acts.name: NDPS Act 1985, Section 20
special_acts.title: Cannabis offences (ganja, hashish)
special_acts.punishment: Small quantity: 1 year / ₹10K; Commercial: 10-20 years + ₹1-2 lakh
special_acts.explanation: Drug possession scaled by quantity.

special_acts.name: NDPS Act 1985, Section 22
special_acts.title: Psychotropic substances offences
special_acts.explanation: MDMA, LSD, etc.

special_acts.name: Prevention of Money Laundering Act 2002, Section 3
special_acts.title: Offence of money laundering
special_acts.punishment: 3-7 years + fine (up to 10 years for narcotic offences)
special_acts.explanation: Concealing proceeds of crime.

special_acts.name: PMLA Act, Section 45
special_acts.title: Twin conditions for PMLA bail
special_acts.explanation: Tougher bail conditions in PMLA cases — upheld in Vijay Madanlal but read down in Tarsem Lal.

special_acts.name: Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act 1967, Section 13
special_acts.title: Unlawful activities
special_acts.explanation: Membership/support of banned organizations.

special_acts.name: UAPA Act, Section 16
special_acts.title: Terrorist act
special_acts.explanation: Acts of terror against India.

special_acts.name: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Section 35
special_acts.title: Filing a consumer complaint
special_acts.punishment: Civil — refund, replacement, compensation, punitive damages
special_acts.explanation: Defective product or deficient service. District Commission up to ₹50L; State up to ₹2Cr; National >₹2Cr.

special_acts.name: Right to Information Act 2005, Section 6
special_acts.title: Request for obtaining information
special_acts.punishment: Civil — info must be provided within 30 days
special_acts.explanation: ₹10 application fee for general; free for BPL. CPIO responds within 30 days.

special_acts.name: RTI Act 2005, Section 7
special_acts.title: Disposal of request
special_acts.explanation: Life/liberty cases: 48 hrs; threat to life: immediate. Else 30 days.

special_acts.name: RTI Act 2005, Section 19
special_acts.title: Appeal
special_acts.explanation: First appeal: 30 days. Second appeal to Information Commission: 90 days.

special_acts.name: Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act 2017
special_acts.title: Tenancy rights TN
special_acts.explanation: Deposit cap = 1 month residential; written rent agreement compulsory; deposit must be returned within 1 month of vacating (TN Act 2017, Sec 5).

special_acts.name: Delhi Rent Control Act 1958
special_acts.title: Tenancy rights Delhi
special_acts.explanation: Applies to properties with rent <₹3,500/month (old). Newer properties under Transfer of Property Act.

special_acts.name: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999
special_acts.title: Tenancy rights Maharashtra
special_acts.explanation: Pagdi system + standard tenancies covered.

special_acts.name: Medical Termination of Pregnancy Act 1971, Section 3
special_acts.title: When pregnancy can be terminated
special_acts.explanation: Up to 20 weeks (1 doctor); 20-24 weeks (2 doctors, certain categories). After 24 weeks: medical board approval. X v Principal Secretary 2022.

special_acts.name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act 2013, Section 9
special_acts.title: Complaint to Internal Committee
special_acts.explanation: Filed within 3 months (extendable to 6). IC must complete inquiry in 90 days.

special_acts.name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023, Section 43
special_acts.title: Data principal rights
special_acts.explanation: Right to access, correct, erase personal data. Consent must be free, specific, informed.

special_acts.name: Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act 1986, Section 3
special_acts.title: Prohibition of children under 14 from employment
special_acts.explanation: Children under 14 cannot work except in family enterprises (non-hazardous, after school).

special_acts.name: SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act 1989, Section 3
special_acts.title: Offences of atrocities
special_acts.explanation: Caste-based discrimination, humiliation, violence. Special courts.

special_acts.name: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act 2007
special_acts.title: Maintenance of parents by children
special_acts.punishment: Up to ₹10,000/month maintenance + 3 months on default
special_acts.explanation: Children legally bound to maintain parents above 60. Application to Maintenance Tribunal.

special_acts.name: Arms Act 1959, Section 25
special_acts.title: Possession of arms without licence
special_acts.explanation: Illegal firearms possession.

special_acts.name: TN Prohibition Act 1937, Section 4
special_acts.title: Prohibition / sale of intoxicants in TN
special_acts.explanation: TN has prohibition (limited to TASMAC). Other liquor manufacture/sale offence.

special_acts.name: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015, Section 75
special_acts.title: Cruelty to child
special_acts.explanation: Causing mental or physical cruelty.

special_acts.name: JJ Act 2015, Section 83
special_acts.title: Use of child for begging / vending illegal substances
special_acts.explanation: Adults using children for begging or selling drugs.
